pubmed_links: [Narita et al. (2020) Clustering by phenotype and genome-wide association study in autism](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32807774/); [Yamagata (2020) Structure and Functions of Sidekicks](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32982686/); [Grove et al. (2019) Identification of common genetic risk variants for autism spectrum disorder](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30804558/)
summary: SDK1 encodes Sidekick-1, a large immunoglobulin superfamily cell adhesion molecule that mediates synaptic specificity and neural circuit formation; phenotype-stratified GWAS in autism have identified SDK1 as a candidate risk gene, and genome-wide association evidence also implicates SDK1 polymorphisms in ADHD, with the gene's large genomic footprint rendering it susceptible to somatic rearrangements potentially contributing to neurodevelopmental vulnerability. (emerging evidence)
